properties.remark: QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED IN WOODBRIDGE.
QUARTER-SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED IN THE TOWN OF NORTH SAN JUAN LATE YESTERDAY EVENING.
TREE BLOWN DOWN ON MOBILE HOME IN THE TOWN OF SHASTA LAKE.
PORTIONS OF EAST GRIDLEY ROAD CLOSED WHEN GUSTY WINDS BROUGHT LOW VISIBILITIES WITH BLOWING DUST.
TREE DOWN/POWER LINES DOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF ALMANOR DRIVE WEST AND POLE LINE ROAD.
REPORT OF FLOODING AND DEBRIS ON PARK LOOP ROAD IN LASSEN NATIONAL PARK BETWEEN LASSEN PEAK AND MANZANITA LAKE.
CHP REPORTS MUD, ROCK, AND DIRT ON ROAD.
1 MILE SW OF BEAR VALLEY AT HELLS KITCHEN
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL AT HELLS KITCHEN VISTA.
BEAR VALLEY.
REPORTS OF BRANCHES AND DEBRIS ALONG STATE HIGHWAY 26 NEAR JESUS MARIA RD IN THE BUTTE FIRE BURN SCAR.
ELECTRA ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO MUDSLIDES.
6 POWER POLES SNAPPED OFF ALONG EAST EATON RD AT GODMAN AVE. 10000 PGE CUSTOMERS WITHOUT POWER.
POSSIBLE TORNADO REPORTED BETWEEN DENAIR AND HUGHSON . TIME ESTIMATE BASED ON RADAR.
VIDEO EVIDENCE & EYEWITNESS REPORTS CONFIRM THERE WAS A TORNADO IN DENAIR THIS AFTERNOON BETWEEN 1:45 PM AND 2:00 PM. A DAMAGE RATING WILL BE MADE AFTER A NWS SURVEY IS DONE TOMORROW (11/16/15)

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF DENAIR CA SUNDAY AFTERNOON. THE TORNADO HAD MULTIPLE TOUCHDOWNS AND LEFT DAMAGE ALONG A 3 MILE LONG PATH. THE ORIGINAL TOUCHDOWN POINT IS OFF BARNHART ROAD, THEN TRAVELED NEARLY DUE EAST TO WARING ROAD.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF DENAIR CA SUNDAY AFTERNOON. THE TORNADO HAD MULTIPLE TOUCHDOWNS AND LEFT DAMAGE ALONG A 3 MILE LONG PATH. THE ORIGINAL TOUCHDOWN POINT IS OFF BARNHART ROAD, THEN TRAVELED NEARLY DUE EAST TO WARING ROAD. DURATION OF ENTIRE TORNADO EVENT WAS APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES WITH AN ENDING TIME OF 215 PM.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
STRONG OUTFLOW WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM DAMAGED A MOBILE HOME IN NEWCASTLE, RIPPING OFF THE OUTER ROOF AND CARPORT.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL COVERING GROUND.
HWY PATROL INCIDENT REPORT OF MUD/ROCK SLIDE ON HWY 50 NEAR WRIGHTS LAKE RD. ONE LANE OBSTRUCTED, TRAFFIC ABLE TO DRIVE AROUND.
MINOR MUD FLOW IN THE KING FIRE BURN AREA AT CAMINO RESERVOIR REPORTED AT JAYBIRD POWER HOUSE.
MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW COVERING ROAD AT CALAVERAS RIVER. ROAD CLOSED UNTIL DEBRIS IS CLEARED.
INVESTIGATING POSSIBLE TORNADO IN MODESTO/CERES WILL ISSUE LOCAL STORM REPORT UPDATE SOON AS POSSIBLE.
STRAIGHT LINE WINDS. TREE DOWN AT COFFEE AND ATHENS ROAD. TREE BROUGHT DOWN 12 POWER POLES. SHALLOW ROOTS ON TREE.
UPDATE TO PREVIOUS LSR: EF0 TORNADO IN CERES ESTIMATED ON THE GROUND AROUND 3:00-3:05 PM PST. DAMAGE BETWEEN DAVIS WAY AND NORTH CENTRAL AVE. TO PARK E DRIVE, AND VINEWOOD DRIVE.

EF1 DAMAGE HAS BEEN CONFIRMED IN EL DORADO HILLS AND CAMERON PARK. SEVERAL RESIDENCES WERE AFFECTED, WITH ROOFS DAMAGED, FENCES BLOWN OVER, AND LARGE TREES UPROOTED. ONE RETAIL BUSINESS SAW MODERATE DAMAGE WITH A GOOD PORTION OF ITS ROOF DESTROYED. THE TORNADO IS NOT BELIEVED TO HAVE BEEN ON THE GROUND IN ITS ENTIRE DURATION.
TORNADO 4 MILES EAST OF BRIGGS RANCH DRIVE IN FOLSOM. DAMAGE AND INTENSITY UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME, WILL FOLLOW UP WITH ANOTHER LOCAL STORM REPORT.

EF0 DAMAGE HAS BEEN CONFIRMED IN CERES FROM DAVIS RD TO VINEWOOD DR. A CARPORT COLLAPSED CAUSING DAMAGE TO A HOUSE. SEVERAL FENCES WERE DAMAGED AND SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED. THE TORNADO WAS ONLY ON THE GROUND FOR A BRIEF TIME.
